domain: Brittle star (Ophiocoma wendtii) calcite double-lens compound eye — entire dorsal skeleton functions as compound eye via large-N microlens array.
specific_mechanism: Large N (>1000) identical small-aperture double-lens elements + globally-modulated pigmentation aperture mask.
llm_application: SKELETON-VIS: vision encoder using a LARGE-N array of identical small-aperture micro-encoders over a heavily overlapping image grid; learned pigmentation gain mask modulates per-aperture activation; results are concatenated as a token bundle. Differs from ViT (single encoder + grid patches) and from MERV-style multi-encoder by large-N + identical + global gain mask.
novelty_claim: ViT processes patches with single encoder. MERV/Cambrian-1 use 3-4 specialist encoders. SKELETON-VIS uses LARGE-N identical micro-encoders over overlapping apertures + global pigmentation gain map.
